title: ActivityConfig.activityRecognitionInterval
text: Interval in milliseconds between motion-activity polls. Defaults to ms (10 seconds). Lower values increase responsiveness to state changes at the cost of additional battery use. The minimum supported  int? activityRecognitionInterval

title: ActivityConfig.disableMotionActivityUpdates
text: Disables the SDK's request for Motion & Fitness (iOS) or Physical Activity (Android 10+) permission. Defaults to . Set to to suppress the permission request. The SDK remains functional but without mot bool? disableMotionActivityUpdates

title: ActivityConfig.disableStopDetection
text: Disables the motion-activity-based stop-detection system. Defaults to . iOS Disables the accelerometer-based stop-detection system. When disabled, the SDK falls back to the default iOS behaviour of au bool? disableStopDetection

title: ActivityConfig.minimumActivityRecognitionConfidence
text: Minimum confidence level (0–100) required before a detected activity triggers a moving/stationary state change. Defaults to on iOS and on Android. Higher values reduce false-positive transitions but m int? minimumActivityRecognitionConfidence

title: ActivityConfig.motionTriggerDelay
text: [Android only] Adds a delay in milliseconds before the SDK commits to the *moving* state when the Motion API reports movement. Defaults to (no delay). When the Motion API reports a moving activity (e. int? motionTriggerDelay

title: ActivityConfig.stopDetectionDelay
text: [iOS only] Delays the iOS stop-detection system from engaging after the device becomes stationary. Defaults to (engage immediately). When the stop-detection system engages, location services are tempo int? stopDetectionDelay

title: ActivityConfig.stopOnStationary
text: Automatically calls { bool? stopOnStationary

title: ActivityConfig.triggerActivities
text: Restricts which detected activities can trigger a transition from the *stationary* state to the *moving* state. By default the SDK triggers on any moving activity: , , , , or . Configure this to limit String? triggerActivities

title: ActivityType.Airborne
text: Airborne activity (iOS 15+). ActivityType.airborne

title: ActivityType.AutomotiveNavigation
text: Automotive navigation mode. ActivityType.automotiveNavigation

title: ActivityType.Fitness
text: Fitness-related activity. ActivityType.fitness

title: ActivityType.Other
text: Default/unspecified activity. ActivityType.other

title: ActivityType.OtherNavigation
text: Non-automotive navigation mode. ActivityType.otherNavigation

title: AppConfig.backgroundPermissionRationale
text: [Android only] Configures the dialog shown when requesting *Always* background location permission on Android 11 and later. Android 11 removed the Allow all the time button from the system permission  PermissionRationale? backgroundPermissionRationale

title: AppConfig.enableHeadless
text: [Android only] Enables headless operation so the SDK can respond to events after the app has been terminated with { bool? enableHeadless

title: AppConfig.heartbeatInterval
text: Rate in seconds at which { int? heartbeatInterval

title: AppConfig.notification
text: [Android only] Configures the persistent foreground-service notification required by Android. ![](https://dl.dropbox.com/s/acuhy5cu4p7uofr/android-foreground-service-default.png?dl=1) See { Notification? notification

title: AppConfig.preventSuspend
text: [iOS only] Prevents iOS from suspending the application after location services have been disabled while running in the background. Defaults to . Set to to keep the app alive in the background even af bool? preventSuspend

title: AppConfig.schedule
text: Configures a cron-like automated schedule that tells the SDK when to { List<String>? schedule

title: AppConfig.scheduleUseAlarmManager
text: [Android only] Forces the Android scheduler to use (exact, on-the-minute) instead of (inexact). Defaults to . bool? scheduleUseAlarmManager

title: AppConfig.startOnBoot
text: Controls whether tracking resumes automatically after the device reboots. Defaults to . iOS iOS cannot begin tracking immediately after a reboot. Similar to { bool? startOnBoot

title: AppConfig.stopOnTerminate
text: Controls whether tracking stops when the application is terminated by the user. Defaults to . When , the SDK calls as the app terminates. Set to to keep tracking alive after termination — iOS and Andr bool? stopOnTerminate

title: AuthorizationConfig.accessToken
text: The access token attached to each HTTP request as an header. The SDK automatically applies the token to every upload request sent to { String? accessToken

title: AuthorizationConfig.expires
text: Token expiry time in seconds since epoch. Defaults to (not set). When set, the SDK proactively refreshes the token before expiry rather than waiting for a response. When not set (default ), the SDK re int? expires

title: AuthorizationConfig.refreshHeaders
text: HTTP headers sent with each request to { Map? refreshHeaders

title: AuthorizationConfig.refreshPayload
text: Form fields sent in the POST to { Map? refreshPayload

title: AuthorizationConfig.refreshToken
text: The token sent to { String? refreshToken

title: AuthorizationConfig.refreshUrl
text: The URL of the authorization server that provides a new { String? refreshUrl

title: AuthorizationConfig.strategy
text: Authorization strategy used when attaching the token to HTTP requests. Defaults to . - — sends the token as . - — sends the token as (no prefix). String? strategy

title: AuthorizationEvent.error
text: Error message when is ; otherwise. String? error

title: AuthorizationEvent.response
text: Decoded JSON body returned by the refresh server when is ; otherwise. Map? response

title: AuthorizationEvent.status
text: HTTP status code returned by { int status

title: AuthorizationEvent.success
text: when the token refresh request returned a 2xx status code. bool success

title: BackgroundGeolocation.onNotificationAction

title: Config.activity

title: Config.geolocation

title: Config.persistence

title: ConnectivityChangeEvent.connected

title: Coords.accuracy
text: Horizontal accuracy radius in meters. double accuracy

title: Coords.altitude
text: Altitude above a reference plane in meters. iOS Altitude above mean sea level. Android Altitude above the WGS84 reference ellipsoid. See also { double altitude

title: Coords.altitude_accuracy
text: Vertical accuracy in meters. Returns if unavailable. iOS When positive, the value is within ± meters. When negative, is invalid. Determining altitude accuracy requires a GPS-capable device; on some de double altitudeAccuracy

title: Coords.ellipsoidal_altitude
text: Altitude above the WGS84 reference ellipsoid in meters. double ellipsoidalAltitude

title: Coords.heading

title: Coords.heading_accuracy
text: Heading accuracy in degrees. Note Only present when the location came from GPS. Returns otherwise. double headingAccuracy

title: Coords.latitude

title: Coords.longitude
text: Longitude in decimal degrees. double longitude

title: Coords.speed
text: Ground speed in meters per second. Note Only present when the location came from GPS. Returns otherwise. double speed

title: Coords.speed_accuracy
text: Speed accuracy in meters per second. Note Only present when the location came from GPS. Returns otherwise. double speedAccuracy

text: Medium accuracy (e.g., 10m). DesiredAccuracy.medium

text: Navigation-level accuracy (e.g., 0-10m). DesiredAccuracy.navigation

title: DesiredAccuracy.VeryLow

title: GeoConfig.activityType
text: [iOS only] Specifies the Core Motion activity type used by iOS to optimize its internal stop-detection algorithm. Apple is intentionally vague about how this affects motion interpretation, but each ac ActivityType? activityType

title: GeoConfig.allowIdenticalLocations
text: [Android only] Allows duplicate locations to be recorded when consecutive fixes are identical. Defaults to . By default, the SDK ignores a location that is identical to the previous one. Set to record bool? allowIdenticalLocations

title: GeoConfig.deferTime
text: [Android only] Sets the maximum wait time in milliseconds before batched location updates are delivered. Defaults to (no deferral). When set to a value at least 2× the { int? deferTime

title: GeoConfig.desiredAccuracy
text: Specifies the desired accuracy of the geolocation system. Defaults to { DesiredAccuracy? desiredAccuracy

title: GeoConfig.disableElasticity
text: Disables automatic speed-based { bool? disableElasticity

title: GeoConfig.disableLocationAuthorizationAlert
text: Disables the SDK's automatic alert when location authorization is insufficient. Defaults to . By default, the SDK shows a native alert directing the user to the Settings screen when location services  bool? disableLocationAuthorizationAlert

title: GeoConfig.distanceFilter
text: The minimum distance (meters) a device must move horizontally before a new location is recorded. Defaults to meters. By default, is elastically auto-scaled by the SDK: when speed increases, increases; double? distanceFilter

title: GeoConfig.elasticityMultiplier
text: Controls the scale of automatic speed-based { double? elasticityMultiplier

title: GeoConfig.enableTimestampMeta
text: Appends extra timestamp metadata to each recorded location, including system time. Defaults to . Some devices report GPS { bool? enableTimestampMeta

title: GeoConfig.fastestLocationUpdateInterval
text: [Android only] Sets the fastest interval for location updates, in milliseconds. Defaults to (not set). When other apps or system components trigger location updates at a faster rate, the SDK receives  int? fastestLocationUpdateInterval

title: GeoConfig.filter
text: Defines how raw GPS samples are filtered, denoised, and smoothed before being recorded or used for odometer calculations. is supplied via { LocationFilter? filter

title: GeoConfig.geofenceInitialTriggerEntry
text: Controls whether a geofence fires an entry event immediately if the device is already inside it when the geofence is registered. Defaults to . Set to suppress the immediate entry trigger and wait unti bool? geofenceInitialTriggerEntry

title: GeoConfig.geofenceModeHighAccuracy
text: [Android only] Enables high-accuracy mode for geofence-only tracking. Defaults to . Runs { bool? geofenceModeHighAccuracy

title: GeoConfig.geofenceProximityRadius
text: Defines the radius (meters) around the device used to query for geofences that should be actively monitored. Mobile platforms allow only a limited number of concurrently monitored geofences (iOS: 20,  int? geofenceProximityRadius

title: GeoConfig.locationAuthorizationAlert
text: [iOS only] Customizes the text displayed in the SDK's location-authorization alert dialog. When { Map<String, dynamic>? locationAuthorizationAlert

title: GeoConfig.locationAuthorizationRequest
text: Declares the location authorization level the app requires from the user. Defaults to . Valid values are: - — background and foreground location access - — foreground location access only - — accept w String? locationAuthorizationRequest

title: GeoConfig.locationUpdateInterval
text: [Android only] Sets the desired interval for active location updates, in milliseconds. Defaults to ms. The system honors this interval on a best-effort basis — updates may arrive slower (no providers  int? locationUpdateInterval

title: GeoConfig.pausesLocationUpdatesAutomatically
text: [iOS only] Prevents the iOS location API from automatically pausing location updates. When set to , iOS location services never turn off automatically. By default (), the SDK manages this automaticall bool? pausesLocationUpdatesAutomatically

title: GeoConfig.showsBackgroundLocationIndicator
text: [iOS only] Controls whether the status bar indicator appears when location services are active in the background with authorization. Defaults to . When , iOS displays a blue bar or pill in the status  bool? showsBackgroundLocationIndicator

title: GeoConfig.stationaryRadius
text: The minimum distance the device must move beyond the stationary location before aggressive background-tracking re-engages. iOS Defaults to meters. In practice, iOS requires approximately 200 meters of int? stationaryRadius

title: GeoConfig.stopAfterElapsedMinutes
text: Automatically stops tracking after the specified number of minutes. Disabled by default. When set, the SDK automatically calls { int? stopAfterElapsedMinutes

title: GeoConfig.stopTimeout
text: Minutes to wait in the *moving* state with no detected movement before transitioning to the *stationary* state. Defaults to minutes. When in the *moving* state, the SDK waits this many minutes after t int? stopTimeout

title: GeoConfig.useSignificantChangesOnly
text: Disables continuous background tracking in favour of periodic, coarse location updates. Defaults to . When , a location is recorded only every 500–1000 meters (higher in non-urban environments, depend bool? useSignificantChangesOnly

title: Geofence.entryState
text: Current entry state of the geofence. | Value | State | |------:|-------| | | Outside | | | Inside | int? entryState

text: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached to each geofence event and included in the payload posted to { Map<String, dynamic>? extras

title: Geofence.hits

title: Geofence.latitude

title: Geofence.loiteringDelay
text: Minimum time in milliseconds the device must remain inside the geofence before a { int? loiteringDelay

title: Geofence.longitude
text: Longitude of the circular geofence center. Omit when defining a polygon geofence via { double? longitude

title: Geofence.vertices

text: The SDK event that triggered this headless task invocation (e.g. , , , , ). String name

title: HeartbeatEvent.location

title: HttpConfig.autoSync
text: Uploads each recorded location to { bool? autoSync

title: HttpConfig.autoSyncThreshold
text: Minimum number of queued records required before an automatic upload fires. Defaults to (no threshold — upload after every recorded location). When set above , the SDK waits until at least this many l int? autoSyncThreshold

title: HttpConfig.batchSync
text: Bundles all queued locations into a single HTTP request. Defaults to . When , the SDK sends all records currently in the SQLite queue in one HTTP request rather than one request per location. Use { bool? batchSync

title: HttpConfig.disableAutoSyncOnCellular
text: Defers automatic uploads until the device is on Wi-Fi. Defaults to . When , { bool? disableAutoSyncOnCellular

title: HttpConfig.headers
text: HTTP headers applied to every outbound upload request. The supplied headers are merged with the SDK's automatically applied headers (including ). When { Map<String, dynamic>? headers

title: HttpConfig.maxBatchSize
text: Maximum number of records included in each batched HTTP request. Defaults to (no limit — all queued records in one request). When { int? maxBatchSize

title: HttpConfig.method
text: The HTTP method used when uploading locations to { String? method

title: HttpConfig.params
text: Key/value pairs merged into the JSON body of every outbound upload request. Params are merged at the root level of the payload, or nested under { Map<String, dynamic>? params

title: HttpConfig.rootProperty
text: Wraps the location payload under a named root key in the JSON body. Defaults to . When set, outgoing payloads nest the serialized String? rootProperty

title: HttpConfig.timeout
text: HTTP request timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to ms (60 seconds). When a request exceeds this duration, the SDK fires { int? timeout

title: HttpConfig.url
text: The server URL where the SDK posts recorded locations. The SDK hosts a robust HTTP service that continuously uploads recorded locations to your server in the background, surviving app termination and  String? url

title: HttpEvent.responseText
text: Raw response body returned by the server. Parse this string to extract server-side data, error messages, or remote-control commands embedded in the response. String responseText

title: HttpEvent.status
text: HTTP status code returned by the server (e.g. , , , ). int status

title: HttpEvent.success
text: when the server returned a 2xx status code. bool success

title: KalmanProfile.Aggressive
text: Aggressive — Fast response; minimal smoothing. KalmanProfile.aggressive

title: KalmanProfile.Conservative
text: Conservative — Maximum smoothing; slowest response to changes. KalmanProfile.conservative

title: KalmanProfile.Default
text: Balanced — General-purpose; suitable for most movement types. KalmanProfile.defaultProfile

title: Location.activity
text: Motion activity detected by the device at the time this location was recorded (e.g. , , ). Activity activity

title: Location.age
text: Age of the location in milliseconds, measured from the device system clock at the time the location was received. + = device system time when the SDK received the location from the native API. double age

title: Location.battery
text: Device battery state at the time this location was recorded. Battery battery

title: Location.coords
text: Geographic coordinates: latitude, longitude, accuracy, speed, heading, and altitude. Coords coords

title: Location.event
text: SDK event that triggered this location record. One of: , , , . String event

title: Location.extras
text: Optional arbitrary metadata attached to this location. Merged with configured { Map? extras

title: Location.geofence
text: The geofence event that triggered this location, if applicable. Present only when { GeofenceEvent? geofence

title: Location.is_moving
text: when the SDK was in the moving state when this location was recorded. bool isMoving

title: Location.mock
text: when the location was generated by a mock location app or simulator. bool mock

title: Location.odometer
text: Accumulated distance traveled in meters since the last odometer reset. The SDK integrates the distance between each pair of accepted locations to maintain a running total. This value survives app rest double odometer

title: Location.recorded_at
text: Timestamp when the SDK received the location from the native API. - When { dynamic recordedAt

title: Location.sample
text: for intermediate sample locations collected during accuracy convergence. The SDK collects multiple samples when transitioning between motion states or during { bool sample

title: Location.timestamp
text: Timestamp provided by the native location API. - When { dynamic timestamp

title: Location.uuid
text: Universally unique identifier for this location record. Use this to correlate locations in your server database with those in the SDK logs, or to detect whether a location has been delivered more than String uuid

title: LocationFilter.burstWindow
text: Duration in seconds of each burst window used for averaging samples. Defaults to seconds. Valid range: 0.2–120. The SDK groups all locations received within this time window into a single averaged out double? burstWindow

title: LocationFilter.filterDebug
text: Enables verbose debug logging for the filtering engine. Defaults to . When enabled, the SDK logs detailed decisions for each incoming sample, including whether it was ACCEPTED, REJECTED, or IGNORED by bool? filterDebug

title: LocationFilter.kalmanDebug
text: Enables verbose Kalman debug output in logs. Defaults to . When enabled, the SDK logs additional diagnostic data about the Kalman filter's internal state (for example, variance and innovation) for eac bool? kalmanDebug

title: LocationFilter.kalmanProfile
text: Selects the preset tuning profile for the Kalman filter. Defaults to . Each profile adjusts the Kalman filter's process and measurement noise parameters, trading off between responsiveness and smoothn KalmanProfile? kalmanProfile

title: LocationFilter.maxBurstDistance
text: Maximum distance in meters between samples considered part of the same burst. Defaults to meters. Valid range: 5–2000. Prevents the filter from merging location samples that are too far apart into a s double? maxBurstDistance

title: LocationFilter.maxImpliedSpeed
text: Maximum implied speed in meters/second permitted before a location sample is rejected. Defaults to m/s (≈ 216 km/h). Valid range: 1–200. If a raw sample implies a speed greater than this threshold — c double? maxImpliedSpeed

title: LocationFilter.odometerAccuracyThreshold
text: Maximum horizontal accuracy in meters allowed for a sample to affect the odometer. Defaults to meters. Valid range: 0–500. Any location whose accuracy exceeds this threshold is ignored for odometer up double? odometerAccuracyThreshold

title: LocationFilter.odometerUseKalmanFilter
text: Applies a Kalman filter to odometer calculations, independent of { bool? odometerUseKalmanFilter

title: LocationFilter.policy
text: Selects the filtering policy applied to incoming raw GPS samples before they are accepted, averaged, or rejected. Defaults to . The filtering policy determines how aggressively the SDK removes noisy,  LocationFilterPolicy? policy

title: LocationFilter.rollingWindow
text: Number of samples in the rolling window used for burst averaging. Defaults to . Valid range: 3–20. Higher values produce smoother averaged locations but introduce greater latency in responsiveness to  int? rollingWindow

title: LocationFilter.trackingAccuracyThreshold
text: Maximum acceptable horizontal accuracy in meters for a sample to qualify for tracking. Defaults to meters. Valid range: 0–500. Locations with an accuracy value greater (worse) than this threshold are  double? trackingAccuracyThreshold

title: LocationFilter.useKalman
text: Enables the Kalman filter to smooth incoming location speed and position. Defaults to . When enabled, the SDK applies a Kalman filter to reduce noise and stabilize velocity and distance calculations. bool? useKalman

title: LocationFilterPolicy.Adjust
text: Balanced (default) — applies moderate filtering to reject noisy samples. Dynamically adjusts acceptance thresholds for incoming samples, but never alters the raw latitude/longitude coordinates. When u LocationFilterPolicy.adjust

title: LocationFilterPolicy.Conservative
text: Aggressive — filters heavily, preferring stability over responsiveness. LocationFilterPolicy.conservative

title: LocationFilterPolicy.PassThrough
text: No filtering — accept all samples. Useful for debugging. LocationFilterPolicy.passThrough

title: LogLevel.Debug
text: Developer-level debug output. LogLevel.debug

title: LogLevel.Error
text: Log only critical failures. LogLevel.error

title: LogLevel.Info
text: Operational information. LogLevel.info

title: LogLevel.Off
text: Disable all logging. LogLevel.off

title: LogLevel.Verbose
text: Maximum detail. LogLevel.verbose

title: LogLevel.Warning
text: Log warnings + errors. LogLevel.warning

title: Logger.debug
text: Insert a debug-level message into the SDK's log database. static Future<void> debug(String msg)

title: Logger.destroyLog
text: Delete all records from the SDK's log database. See also - { static Future<bool> destroyLog()

title: Logger.emailLog
text: Send the SDK's log database to an email address via the device mail client. Provide an optional { static Future<bool> emailLog(String email, [SQLQuery? query])

title: Logger.error
text: Insert an error-level message into the SDK's log database. static Future<void> error(String msg)

title: Logger.getLog
text: Fetch the SDK's log database as a string. Provide an optional { static Future<String> getLog([SQLQuery? query])

title: Logger.info
text: Insert an info-level message into the SDK's log database. static Future<void> info(String msg)

title: Logger.notice
text: Insert a notice-level message into the SDK's log database. static Future<void> notice(String msg)

title: Logger.uploadLog
text: Upload the SDK's log database to a URL as a gzipped multipart file. Provide an optional { static Future<bool> uploadLog(String url, [SQLQuery? query])

title: Logger.warn
text: Insert a warning-level message into the SDK's log database. static Future<void> warn(String msg)

title: LoggerConfig.debug
text: Enables audible soundFX and debug notifications during development. When , the SDK plays a distinct sound for each key lifecycle event so you can follow SDK activity without watching the console. It m bool? debug

title: LoggerConfig.logLevel
text: Controls the verbosity of the SDK's internal log. Defaults to { LogLevel? logLevel

title: LoggerConfig.logMaxDays
text: Number of days to retain log entries in the internal SQLite database. Defaults to days. Entries older than this limit are purged automatically on a rolling basis. See also - { int? logMaxDays

title: MotionActivity.confidence
text: Confidence of the reported activity as a percentage (0–100). int confidence

title: MotionActivity.type
text: Detected motion activity type (e.g. , , ). String type

title: NotificationConfig.actions
text: Declare click listeners for elements of a custom notification { List<String>? actions

title: NotificationConfig.channelId
text: Identifier of the Android notification channel used for the foreground service notification. Defaults to . Note Changing this is not typically required. A use case is sharing an existing notification  String? channelId

title: NotificationConfig.channelName
text: Name of the Android notification channel used for the foreground service notification. Defaults to . On Android O and above, foreground services require a notification channel. The channel name is vis String? channelName

title: NotificationConfig.color
text: Accent color of the notification icon. Not set by default. Applies to API level 21 and above. Supported formats: - - String? color

title: NotificationConfig.largeIcon
text: Large icon for the foreground notification. Not set by default. ⚠️ Warning - Specify the resource type ( or ) followed by the icon name in the format . - Do not include the file extension (e.g. ). See String? largeIcon

title: NotificationConfig.layout
text: Name of a custom Android Layout XML file for the foreground notification. See also - [Android Custom Notification Layout](github:wiki/Android-Custom-Notification-Layout) ![](https://dl.dropbox.com/s/w String? layout

title: NotificationConfig.priority
text: Controls the position and visibility of the foreground notification in the system shade. Defaults to (). affects both the ordering of the notification in the notification shade and the position/visibi NotificationPriority? priority

title: NotificationConfig.smallIcon
text: Small status-bar icon for the foreground notification. Defaults to (the app launcher icon). ⚠️ Warning - Specify the resource type ( or ) followed by the icon name in the format . - Do not include the String? smallIcon

title: NotificationConfig.sticky
text: Keeps the foreground service notification visible at all times, regardless of motion state. Defaults to . By default the notification is shown only while the SDK detects the device is moving. Set to t bool? sticky

title: NotificationConfig.strings
text: Custom strings to render into elements of a custom notification { Map<String, String>? strings

title: NotificationConfig.text
text: Body text of the foreground service notification. Defaults to . String? text

title: NotificationConfig.title
text: Title of the foreground service notification. Defaults to . String? title

title: NotificationPriority.Default
text: Default notification priority (normal weighting). NotificationPriority.defaultPriority

title: NotificationPriority.High
text: Notification strongly weighted to top of list; icon strongly weighted to the left. NotificationPriority.high

title: NotificationPriority.Low
text: Notification weighted to bottom of list; icon weighted to the right. NotificationPriority.low

title: NotificationPriority.Max
text: Same as { NotificationPriority.max

title: NotificationPriority.Min
text: Notification strongly weighted to bottom of list; icon hidden. NotificationPriority.min

title: PermissionRationale.message
text: Body text of the dialog. Explain why your app needs background location access, similar in purpose to the iOS . String? message

title: PermissionRationale.negativeAction
text: Text to display on the negative action button (e.g. "Cancel"). String? negativeAction

title: PermissionRationale.positiveAction
text: Text to display on the positive action button. String? positiveAction

title: PermissionRationale.title
text: Title for the Android background location permission dialog. String? title

title: PersistMode.All
text: Persist both location and geofence data. PersistMode.all

title: PersistMode.Geofence
text: Persist geofence data only. PersistMode.geofence

title: PersistMode.Location
text: Persist location data only. PersistMode.location

title: PersistMode.None
text: Do not persist any data. PersistMode.none

title: PersistenceConfig.disableProviderChangeRecord
text: [Android only] Disables the automatic insertion of a synthetic "provider-change" location into the SDK's SQLite database and its subsequent HTTP upload. By default, when an { bool? disableProviderChangeRecord

title: PersistenceConfig.extras
text: Optional arbitrary key/value pairs merged into each recorded location at write time. These values are persisted and included in all HTTP uploads, making them ideal for attaching static contextual meta Map<String, dynamic>? extras

title: PersistenceConfig.geofenceTemplate
text: Optional custom ERB template for reshaping { String? geofenceTemplate

title: PersistenceConfig.locationTemplate
text: Optional custom ERB template for reshaping { String? locationTemplate

title: PersistenceConfig.locationsOrderDirection
text: Sort order used when selecting records for upload or returning them from { String? locationsOrderDirection

title: PersistenceConfig.maxDaysToPersist
text: Maximum number of days to retain a persisted record in the SDK's on-device SQLite database. Defaults to day. When your server fails to return a response, the SDK continues retrying uploads. If a recor int? maxDaysToPersist

title: PersistenceConfig.maxRecordsToPersist
text: Maximum number of records the SDK may retain in its on-device SQLite database. Defaults to (no limit). When a limit is set and the stored record count would exceed it, the oldest records are purged to int? maxRecordsToPersist

title: PersistenceConfig.persistMode
text: Controls which event types the SDK writes to its internal SQLite database. Defaults to { PersistMode? persistMode

title: PersistenceConfig.timestampFormat
text: Controls the format of the field in the persisted and uploaded location JSON. Because { final String? timestampFormat

title: ProviderChangeEvent.accuracyAuthorization
text: Precision location authorization level. Use the { int accuracyAuthorization

title: ProviderChangeEvent.enabled
text: when the device has at least one location provider enabled. bool enabled

title: ProviderChangeEvent.gps
text: when the GPS-based location provider is available. bool gps

title: ProviderChangeEvent.network
text: when the network-based location provider is available. bool network

title: ProviderChangeEvent.status
text: Platform authorization status code. Use the { int status

title: SQLQuery.end
text: End of the query window (unix timestamp in milliseconds). DateTime? end

title: SQLQuery.limit
text: Maximum number of records to return. int? limit

title: SQLQuery.order
text: Sort order for results: (oldest first) or (newest first). int? order

title: SQLQuery.start
text: Start of the query window (unix timestamp in milliseconds). DateTime? start

title: Sensors.accelerometer
text: when the device has an accelerometer. bool accelerometer

title: Sensors.gyroscope
text: when the device has a gyroscope. bool gyroscope

title: Sensors.magnetometer
text: when the device has a magnetometer (compass). bool magentometer

title: Sensors.motion_hardware
text: [iOS only] when the device has a dedicated M-series motion co-processor (iPhone 5S and later). The M-series co-processor offloads motion activity recognition from the main CPU, enabling low-power step bool? motionHardware

title: Sensors.platform
text: OS platform name: or . String platform

title: Sensors.significant_motion
text: [Android only] when the device supports the Significant Motion hardware trigger. When present, this sensor allows the SDK to wake from a deep idle state only when the device actually moves, saving sig bool? significantMotion

title: State.activity
text: Motion recognition, stop-detection triggers, and motion-trigger delay. See { ActivityConfig get activity

title: State.app
text: App lifecycle — background operation, boot behaviour, headless mode, and foreground notification. See { AppConfig get app

title: State.didDeviceReboot
text: when the app was launched after a device reboot. bool didDeviceReboot

title: State.didLaunchInBackground
text: [iOS only] when the app was relaunched in the background by the OS — for example, due to a background fetch, geofence exit, or stationary geofence transition. On Android, background launch is handled  bool didLaunchInBackground

title: State.enabled
text: when the SDK is actively tracking — i.e. { bool enabled

title: State.geolocation
text: Accuracy, sampling, elasticity, stop-detection, permissions, and geofencing. See { GeoConfig get geolocation

title: State.http
text: Upload URL, HTTP method, sync cadence, batching, headers, and params. See { HttpConfig get http

title: State.isFirstBoot
text: on the very first launch after the app is installed. bool isFirstBoot

title: State.isMoving
text: when the SDK is in the moving state (location services active); when stationary. bool? isMoving

title: State.logger
text: Debug logging, log level, and log retention. See { LoggerConfig get logger

title: State.odometer
text: Accumulated distance traveled since the last odometer reset, in meters. See also - { double odometer

title: State.persistence
text: SQLite storage, TTL, record limits, and custom extras. See { PersistenceConfig get persistence

title: State.schedulerEnabled
text: when a { bool schedulerEnabled

title: State.trackingMode
text: Current tracking mode. | Value | Mode | Description | |------:|------|-------------| | | Geofences | Geofence monitoring only — no active location tracking. | | | Location | Location tracking and geof int trackingMode

title: TransistorAuthorizationService.destroy
text: Remove the cached token associated with the given tracker URL. static Future<bool?> destroy(String url)

title: TransistorAuthorizationService.findOrCreate
text: Find or create an authorization token for the given organization and username. If a token already exists in the local cache for this + combination, the cached token is returned. Otherwise a new token  static Future<TransistorAuthorizationToken> findOrCreate( String orgname, String username, [String url = _DEFAULT_TRANSISTOR_URL])

title: TransistorAuthorizationToken.accessToken
text: JWT access token sent as on each upload. String accessToken

title: TransistorAuthorizationToken.expires
text: Expiry time of the access token (epoch milliseconds). Typically used to populate { int expires

title: TransistorAuthorizationToken.refreshToken
text: JWT refresh token presented to the { String refreshToken

title: TransistorAuthorizationToken.url
text: Base URL of the tracker server that issued this token. String url
